experiences.title: AI & Data Science Trainee
experiences.description:
Selected participant in the national Digital Egypt Pioneers Initiative (DEPI) — AI & Data Science track designed by IBM in partnership with Egypt’s Ministry of Communications & Information Technology (MCIT). Applied the full data-science lifecycle to real problems: data collection and cleaning, exploratory data analysis, feature engineering, model development and evaluation, and production-oriented deployment.

I developed practical skills in the Python data-science ecosystem and SQL, and used MLOps tools (MLflow, Hugging Face) to build reproducible pipelines. I practised prompt engineering and integrated AI tools for automation and model augmentation. Throughout the program I focused on translating data into actionable business insights to improve performance, increase efficiency, and enhance customer experience.

Core coursework and hands-on modules included:
• Prompt Engineering
• What is Data Science? / Data Science Methodology
• Tools for Data Science
• Python for Data Science, AI & Development + Python Project
• Databases & SQL for Data Science with Python
• Data Analysis, Visualization & Machine Learning with Python
• MLOps tools (MLflow, Hugging Face)
• Capstone project: end-to-end AI project demonstrating preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection, evaluation and deployment.

Forward-looking emphasis: reproducible, ethical, and scalable ML/AI solutions that bridge technical results and business impact.

top_repos.name: SmartCV
top_repos.description: AI-powered career assistant — CV parsing, gap analysis, ATS-scored tailored resumes, and outreach automation. Built with Django 5.2, PostgreSQL (Supabase + pgvector), and Groq.
top_repos.readme_excerpt:
# SmartCV

[![Python 3.12+](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.12%2B-blue.svg)](https://www.python.org/downloads/)
[![Django 5.2](https://img.shields.io/badge/django-5.2-092e20.svg)](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)](LICENSE)
[![Tests: 337 passing](https://img.shields.io/badge/tests-337%20passing-brightgreen.svg)](#benchmarks--test-results)
[![Coverage: 53%](https://img.shields.io/badge/coverage-53%25-yellow.svg)](#benchmarks--test-results)

AI-powered career assistant for job seekers. Upload a CV, paste a job
description, and get a gap analysis, an ATS-scored tailored resume, and
an outreach campaign plan — backed by an LLM pipeline that reuses the
same services everywhere instead of one-off scripts.

Built with Django 5.2, PostgreSQL (Supabase + pgvector), Tailwind CSS v4,
and Groq (`meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct`).


![SmartCV dashboard — master profile, profile-strength score, and quick links to job tailoring](docs/images/dashboard.png)

## Highlights

- **Deterministic ATS scoring** with stuffing detection and in-context
  bonus (`resumes/services/scoring.py`).
- **Two-phase gap analysis** — LLM categorization + programmatic
  reconciliation guarantees every JD skill lands in matched / missing /
  partial (`analysis/services/gap_analyzer.py`).
- **CV parsing** with structured Pydantic output and personal-info
  extraction (`profiles/services/cv_parser.py`).
- **Outreach automation** via a Chrome extension that auto-discovers
  LinkedIn targets and drafts personalised messages
  (`extension-outreach/`).
- **Built-in observability** — per-route latency middleware,
  `/healthz/metrics` endpoint, structured request logging.

## Screenshots

### Resume editor with live preview and ATS score


![Resume editor with live preview and ATS score](docs/images/resume-editor.png)

Edit a tailored resume on the left, watch the print-ready preview update on
the right, and see the ATS score recompute as you type.

### Gap analysis


![Gap analysis output — Matched / Critical missing / Soft gaps, with action plan](docs/images/gap-analysis.png)

Two-phase pipeline: an LLM categorises every JD skill, then programmatic
fuzzy reconciliation guarantees every skill lands in `matched`, `partial`,
or `missing` — no silent drops.

### Outreach campaign builder


![Outreach campaign builder — Run a campaign at <Company>, paired with the Chrome extension](docs/images/outreach-campaign.png)

Per-job campaign page that pairs with a Chrome extension. The extension
discovers reachable people from inside your own LinkedIn tab and queues
personalised connect-with-note drafts for review — never automated sending.

## Benchmarks & Test Results

398 Django tests passing. Coverage 53% overall (76.9% in `core/`).

The repo ships a small, real evaluation suite under `benchmarks/` — every
metric has a sample size, a re-run command, and a JSON artifact. No
fabricated numbers. Latest run (2026-04-27, af…

top_repos.name: healthcare-prediction-depi
top_repos.description: DEPI team capstone — stroke risk prediction on Kaggle Stroke dataset (5,110 patients, 4.9% stroke rate). Class-weighted Logistic Regression with 84% recall on stroke cases, Flask REST API. My contribution: data cleaning and EDA.
top_repos.readme_excerpt:
# Stroke Prediction — End-to-End ML Pipeline

> DEPI (Digital Egypt Pioneers Initiative) team capstone. Predicts stroke risk from clinical + lifestyle features on the Kaggle Stroke Prediction dataset (5,110 patients, 4.9% positive class). Full pipeline: data cleaning, EDA, feature engineering, imbalanced classification modeling, and a Flask REST API for predictions.

![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3776AB?logo=python&logoColor=white)
![Pandas](https://img.shields.io/badge/Pandas-150458?logo=pandas&logoColor=white)
![scikit-learn](https://img.shields.io/badge/scikit--learn-F7931E?logo=scikitlearn&logoColor=white)
![Flask](https://img.shields.io/badge/Flask-000000?logo=flask&logoColor=white)
![MLflow](https://img.shields.io/badge/MLflow-0194E2?logo=mlflow&logoColor=white)
![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green)

---

## 👥 Team

This is a **team project**. My contribution: **data cleaning & EDA** (`notebooks/eda.ipynb`).

| Contributor | GitHub | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Zeyad Elshenawy | [@ZeyadElshenawy](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy) | Data cleaning & EDA |
| Forge | [@forge34](https://github.com/forge34) | Preprocessing pipeline, modeling, API, visualization |
| Mahmoud Ebrahim | [@Mahmoudebrahim156](https://github.com/Mahmoudebrahim156) | Project setup |
| Mohamed Ashraf | [@MohamedAchraf22](https://github.com/MohamedAchraf22) | PR contributions |

**Original team repo:** [Mahmoudebrahim156/healthcare-prediction-depi](https://github.com/Mahmoudebrahim156/healthcare-prediction-depi). This is a polished mirror with expanded documentation and extracted EDA plots.

---

## 🎯 Key results

| Metric | No stroke (n=969) | Stroke (n=50) |
|---|---:|---:|
| Precision | 98.9% | 16.5% |
| Recall | 78.0% | **84.0%** |
| F1 | 87.3% | 27.5% |

**Model:** Logistic Regression (L1 regularization, C=0.3, `class_weight='balanced'`, saga solver). Decision threshold tuned to **0.6027** via precision–recall curve analysis.

**Why this is strong:** for stroke *screening*, recall (catching actual positives) matters far more than precision (avoiding false alarms). **84% recall** means the model catches 84 of every 100 stroke cases — an appropriate operating point for a triage tool that would route high-risk patients to further clinical assessment.

---

## 📊 Dataset

**Kaggle Stroke Prediction Dataset** (fedesoriano) — 5,110 patients × 12 features: `age`, `gender`, `hypertension`, `heart_disease`, `ever_married`, `work_type`, `Residence_type`, `avg_glucose_level`, `bmi`, `smoking_status`, `stroke`.

**Heavy class imbalance:** 4,861 no-stroke (95.1%) vs 249 stroke (4.9%). This shapes every downstream modeling decision — stratified splits, class weighting, threshold tuning, and recall as the headline metric.

---

## 🔍 EDA findings

> All plots below are regenerated from `datasets/raw/dataset.csv` — run `python generate_figures.py` to reproduce them.

### Class imbalance
![Class imbalance](figures/class-imbalance.png)

Only **4.9%**…

top_repos.name: customer-segmentation-rfmt
top_repos.description: Customer segmentation on UCI Online Retail — RFMT metrics + K-Means (3 segments: Champions / Promising / At Risk). Jupyter notebook, Streamlit live predictor, and Power BI dashboard.
top_repos.readme_excerpt:
# Customer Segmentation — RFMT + K-Means Clustering

> End-to-end customer segmentation on the **UCI Online Retail** dataset (~541K transactions from a UK online retailer, Dec 2010 – Dec 2011). Extends classical RFM analysis with **Tenure** (RFMT), applies **K-Means clustering**, and delivers a **Jupyter analysis**, an **interactive Power BI dashboard**, and a **Streamlit live predictor**.

![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.9+-3776AB?logo=python&logoColor=white)
![scikit-learn](https://img.shields.io/badge/scikit--learn-F7931E?logo=scikitlearn&logoColor=white)
![Pandas](https://img.shields.io/badge/Pandas-150458?logo=pandas&logoColor=white)
![Plotly](https://img.shields.io/badge/Plotly-3F4F75?logo=plotly&logoColor=white)
![Streamlit](https://img.shields.io/badge/Streamlit-FF4B4B?logo=streamlit&logoColor=white)
![Power BI](https://img.shields.io/badge/Power_BI-F2C811?logo=powerbi&logoColor=black)
![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green)

---

## 🎯 Highlights

- **k=3 validated** — silhouette peaks at **0.351** (vs 0.298 for k=4), confirming three as the right cluster count
- **RFMT methodology** — extends classical RFM with **Tenure** (days as customer) for richer lifecycle signals
- **Three deliverables** — notebook analysis, Power BI dashboard, live Streamlit predictor
- **One segment drives 84% of revenue** — the **Promising** cluster (38% of customers) concentrates the bulk of spend (classic Pareto)
- **76% of At Risk customers at Critical churn risk** — immediate re-engagement needed
- **Cohort analysis** with retention heatmaps to frame the clustering work

## 🧩 Segment profiles

Values below are **means** computed from `customer_segments_analysis.csv` (4,269 customers total).

| Segment | Customers | Recency | Frequency | Monetary | Tenure | Key marketing play |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| ⭐ **Promising** | 1,634 (38%) | 30 d | 188 | $4,412 | 274 d | **Drives 84% of revenue.** Protect with VIP program, early access, retention offers |
| 👑 **Champions** | 948 (22%) | 34 d | 34 | $481 | 50 d | Active newer customers — onboarding, welcome offers, nurture toward Promising |
| ⚠️ **At Risk** | 1,687 (40%) | 174 d | 27 | $564 | 241 d | **76% at Critical churn risk.** Win-back campaigns, reactivation offers |

## 🧪 Methodology

1. **Load & clean** — UCI Online Retail (~541K rows); drop returns, invalid IDs, zero-price rows
2. **Cohort analysis** — monthly cohorts, retention heatmap, average-revenue-per-cohort evolution
3. **Compute RFMT** per customer — Recency, Frequency, Monetary, Tenure
4. **Traditional RFM baseline** — quartile scoring → Gold / Silver / Bronze (for comparison)
5. **Preprocess for K-Means** — `log(x + 1)` to handle right skew, then `StandardScaler`
6. **Elbow method** — evaluate k ∈ [2, 10]
7. **Cluster** with k=3 (compared against k=4 via silhouette)
8. **Profile** — snake plots, relative-importance heatmaps, 3D scatter, sunburst
9. **Business interpretation** — name segments, comp…

top_repos.name: BookShop
top_repos.description: Static bookstore landing page — HTML/CSS/JS, Swiper carousels, client-side cart, demo signup UI.
top_repos.readme_excerpt: # BookShop A static bookstore landing page — HTML/CSS/JavaScript front-end with a Swiper-based book carousel, responsive layout, a client-side cart, and a demo login/signup page. CoCreated with And Forked from [mohaaHeiba/BookShop](https://github.com/mohaaHeiba/BookShop) and cleaned up: filenames normalized (no spaces, no weird casing), broken references fixed, `showMore()` toggle fixed, duplicate `window.onload` removed, and the third-party signup endpoint that was exposing names/emails/plaintext passwords to a public Google Sheet was removed. ## 📸 Screenshots ### Home ### Deal of the day ### Client reviews ### Contact us ### Login & Sign up ## Pages - `index.html` — home (header, book slider, collection, newsletter, deal, reviews, contact, footer) - `product-details.html` — book details view with a thumbnail carousel - `login.html` — login / signup UI (demo only; no backend wired up) ## Project structure ``` BookShop/ ├── index.html ├── product-details.html ├── login.html ├── css/ │ ├── style.css # home + shared │ ├── product-details.css │ └── login.css ├── js/ │ └── script.js # search toggle, cart, loader, Swiper init ├── images/ │ ├── books/ # book covers + interstellar/OIP │ ├── reviews/ # review avatars │ ├── bg/ # section backgrounds │ └── ui/ # loader, decorative GIFs └── screenshots/ # UI screenshots (referenced in README) ``` ## Stack - HTML, CSS, vanilla JavaScript (no build step) - [Swiper](https://swiperjs.com/) for carousels - [Font Awesome](https://fontawesome.com/) for icons - Google Fonts (Poppins) ## Run locally Open `index.html` in a browser, or serve the folder: ```bash python -m http.server 8000 # then visit http://localhost:8000 ``` ## Notes - The signup form in `login.html` is UI-only. It previously POSTed to a public SheetDB endpoint that recorded usernames, emails, and plaintext passwords to a Google Sheet; that endpoint has been removed. Wire up your own backend before re-enabling. - Cart state is in-memory; refreshing the page clears it.

top_repos.name: hr-analytics-dashboard
top_repos.description: Power BI dashboard on the Atlas Labs HR dataset (1,470 employees). Star-schema model + DAX measures for attrition, compensation, and performance.
top_repos.readme_excerpt:
# HR Analytics Dashboard — Atlas Labs

An end-to-end Power BI dashboard analyzing employee attrition, demographics, and performance for **Atlas Labs**, a fictional tech company with 1,470 employees across Technology, Sales, and Human Resources. Built on the Atlas Labs HR dataset (commonly used in DataCamp's *Data Analyst in Power BI* track and Maven Analytics courses).

The dashboard surfaces four executive views — Overview, Demographics, Performance Tracker, and Attrition — driven by a star-schema data model and DAX measures for time-intelligence and rate calculations.

---

## Dashboard Preview

### Overview
High-level headcount KPIs, hiring trends since 2012, and active-employee distribution by department and role.

![Overview](screenshots/Overview.png)

### Demographics
Age, gender, marital status, and ethnicity breakdowns — including average salary by ethnicity.

![Demographics](screenshots/Demographics.png)

### Performance Tracker
Per-employee deep-dive with satisfaction scores and rating history over time. Drill into any employee via the slicer in the top-left.

![Performance Tracker](screenshots/Performance%20Tracker.png)

### Attrition
Attrition rates sliced by department, job role, business travel, overtime requirement, hire date, and tenure.

![Attrition](screenshots/Attrition.png)

---

## Headline Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total Employees | 1,470 |
| Active Employees | 1,233 |
| Inactive (Left) | 237 |
| Attrition Rate | 16.1% |
| Departments | Technology, Sales, Human Resources |
| Hire Date Range | 2012 – 2022 |

---

## Business Questions Answered

- What is our overall attrition rate, and how is it trending?
- Which departments and job roles are losing the most people?
- Do employees who travel frequently or work overtime leave at higher rates?
- Is attrition concentrated in early-tenure employees, or evenly distributed?
- How does pay vary across ethnic groups?
- Which employees are due for performance reviews next, and how have their satisfaction scores changed over time?

---

## Data Model

A classic **star schema** with one fact table and four dimensions:

```
                ┌───────────────────┐
                │   DimEmployee     │
                │  (1,470 rows)     │
                └─────────┬─────────┘
                          │
┌──────────────┐          │          ┌──────────────────┐
│  DimDate     │──────────┼──────────│  DimRatingLevel  │
│ (calculated) │          │          └──────────────────┘
└──────────────┘          │
                          │          ┌──────────────────────┐
                ┌─────────┴─────────┐ │ DimSatisfiedLevel   │
                │FactPerformanceRate│─└──────────────────────┘
                └───────────────────┘
                          │
                ┌─────────┴─────────┐
                │ DimEducationLevel │
                └───────────────────┘
```

### Tables

| Table | Type | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `DimEmployee` | Dimension | `Employee.csv` | 1…

top_repos.name: apotheosis-traffic-sign-detection
top_repos.description: Traffic-sign detection with classical computer vision (OpenCV preprocessing + contour detection). Course project.
top_repos.readme_excerpt:
# 🛣️ Apotheosis: Traffic Sign Detection Project

**Apotheosis** is an image processing project designed to detect and classify traffic signs using computer vision techniques. It uses Python, OpenCV, and Jupyter Notebooks to build and demonstrate a modular detection pipeline.

---

## 📁 Project Structure
```
apotheosis/
├── assets/
│   └── traffic_sign.png            # Sample image
├── output/                         # Output images or result files
├── TEAM.txt                        # Team members information
├── test.ipynb                      # Notebook for testing code or parameters
├── thresholds.json                 # Config file with detection/classification thresholds
├── traffic_sign_detection.ipynb    # Main notebook for traffic sign detection
└── Traffic_Sign_Detection_Report.pdf  # Final report summarizing methodology and results
```

---

## 🚀 Features

- Preprocessing of traffic sign images (color filtering, contour detection, etc.)
- Sign classification using visual characteristics
- Threshold tuning via external JSON configuration
- Clear visualization of results using Jupyter notebooks
- Project documentation and methodology in PDF format

---

## 🛠️ Technologies Used

- Python 3.x
- OpenCV
- Numpy
- Jupyter Notebook

---

## 📦 Installation

Clone this repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/apotheosis-traffic-sign-detection.git
cd apotheosis-traffic-sign-detection
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## 🚀 Quick Start

### Basic Usage

```python
# Load and process an image
image_path = 'assets/traffic_sign.png'
image = cv2.imread(image_path)

# Convert to HSV
hsv_image = cv2.cvtColor(image, cv2.COLOR_BGR2HSV)

# Apply preprocessing
preprocessed = preprocess_image(image)

# Detect traffic signs
detected_signs = detect_traffic_signs(preprocessed, thresholds)
```

### Interactive Parameter Tuning

The notebook provides interactive sliders for real-time parameter adjustment:

```python
# HSV threshold sliders
h_min_slider = widgets.IntSlider(min=0, max=180, step=1, value=h_min_default)
s_min_slider = widgets.IntSlider(min=0, max=255, step=1, value=s_min_default)
v_min_slider = widgets.IntSlider(min=0, max=255, step=1, value=v_min_default)

# Interactive processing
interact(process_image,
         image=fixed(normalized_image),
         h_min=h_min_slider,
         s_min=s_min_slider,
         # ... other parameters
        )
```

## ⚙️ Configuration

### HSV Threshold Ranges

| Parameter | Range | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| Hue | 0-180 | Color tone (OpenCV uses 0-180 for Hue) |
| Saturation | 0-255 | Color intensity |
| Value | 0-255 | Brightness |

### Color Classification

| Color | BGR Ranges | Common Signs |
|-------|------------|--------------|
| Red | R>200, G<80, B<80 | Stop signs, prohibitory signs |
| Yellow | R>200, G>200, B<80 | Warning signs |
| Blue | B>200, R<100, G<100 | Information signs |
| White | R>200, G>200, B>200 | Regulatory signs |

### Shape Classification

| S…

![SAP](https://img.shields.io/badge/SAP-0FAAFF?style=for-the-badge&logo=sap&logoColor=white) ![Git](https://img.shields.io/badge/Git-F05032?style=for-the-badge&logo=git&logoColor=white) ![Django](https://img.shields.io/badge/Django-092E20?style=for-the-badge&logo=django&logoColor=white) ![Flask](https://img.shields.io/badge/Flask-000000?style=for-the-badge&logo=flask&logoColor=white) ![Streamlit](https://img.shields.io/badge/Streamlit-FF4B4B?style=for-the-badge&logo=streamlit&logoColor=white) ![DAX](https://img.shields.io/badge/DAX-F2C811?style=for-the-badge&logo=powerbi&logoColor=black) ![IoT](https://img.shields.io/badge/IoT-00979D?style=for-the-badge&logo=internetofthings&logoColor=white) --- ## 📈 GitHub Stats
![Zeyad's GitHub stats](https://github-readme-stats.vercel.app/api?username=ZeyadElshenawy&show_icons=true&theme=radical&hide_border=true&bg_color=0D1117) ![Top Langs](https://github-readme-stats.vercel.app/api/top-langs/?username=ZeyadElshenawy&layout=compact&theme=radical&hide_border=true&bg_color=0D1117) ![GitHub Streak](https://github-readme-streak-stats.herokuapp.com/?user=ZeyadElshenawy&theme=radical&hide_border=true&background=0D1117)
--- ## 🚀 Featured Projects ### 🤖 SmartCV — AI-powered Career Assistant [![Repo](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-View_Repository-blue?style=flat-square&logo=github)](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/SmartCV) [![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/tests-337%20passing-brightgreen?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/SmartCV#benchmarks--test-results) [![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/badge/coverage-53%25-yellow?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/SmartCV#benchmarks--test-results) Full Django app — upload a CV, paste a JD, and get a gap analysis, an ATS-scored tailored resume, and an outreach-campaign plan paired with a Chrome extension. Backed by Groq llama-4-scout via LangChain through a centralized Pydantic-structured-output gateway. **Ships a real evaluation suite — every public number has a sample size and a re-run command.** Headline benchmarks (2026-04-25): - ATS scoring deterministic, matched-vs-mismatched **Cohen's d 6.27** - Gap analyzer coverage **0.999** (49/50 pairs at 100%) - Skill-extractor F1 **0.81** (hallucination_rate 0.24, after a prompt + JD-anchoring filter pass) - CV parser skills F1 **0.43** on CVs with explicit skills sections (after a PDF-noise sanitizer pass) - Endpoint warm p95 **≤ 13 ms** **Tech Stack:** Django 5.2, Python 3.12, Groq llama-4-scout, LangChain, Pydantic, PostgreSQL (Supabase + pgvector), Tailwind CSS v4, xhtml2pdf, Chrome Extension (manifest v3) --- ### 🎯 Customer Segmentation — RFMT + K-Means Clustering [![Repo](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-View_Repository-blue?style=flat-square&logo=github)](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/customer-segmentation-rfmt) End-to-end customer segmentation on the UCI Online Retail dataset (~541K transactions). Extends classical RFM with **Tenure** (RFMT), applies K-Means clustering (k=3 validated against k=4 via silhouette scores), and delivers three artifacts: a 13-section Jupyter analysis with cohort retention heatmaps, an interactive Power BI dashboard, and a Streamlit live predictor. Identified three actionable segments — **Champions**, **Promising**, **At Risk** — with a concrete marketing playbook per segment. **Tech Stack:** Python, scikit-learn, pandas, Plotly, Streamlit, Power BI --- ### 🧠 Stroke Prediction — Healthcare ML Pipeline [![Repo](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-View_Repository-blue?style=flat-square&logo=github)](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/healthcare-prediction-depi) DEPI team capstone predicting stroke risk from clinical and lifestyle features on the Kaggle Stroke Prediction dataset (5,110 patients, 4.9% positive class). **My contribution: data cleaning & EDA**. The team's final class-weighted Logistic Regression reaches **84% recall on stroke cases** — the clinically important metric for a screening tool — via L1-regularized logistic regression with tuned decision threshold and a Flask REST API for serving. **Tech Stack:** Python, pandas, scikit-learn, Flask, MLflow, Jupyter --- ### 🔬 Brain Tumor Classification — The Magnificent Three [![Repo](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-View_Repository-blue?style=flat-square&logo=github)](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/the-magnificent-three) Cross-platform application for brain tumor classification from MRI scans (Meningioma, Glioma, Pituitary). Team project — I owned the ML pipeline, training and tuning six classical models (SVC, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, KNN, Decision Tree, Naive Bayes) and exposing them via a Flask inference API consumed by a Flutter desktop app and Streamlit dashboard. **Tech Stack:** Python, scikit-learn, Flask, Flutter, Streamlit --- ### 📉 HR Analytics Dashboard (Atlas Labs) [![Repo](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-View_Repository-blue?style=flat-square&logo=github)](https://github.com/ZeyadElshenawy/hr-analytics-dashboard) Power BI dashboard on the Atlas Labs HR dataset (1,470 employees) across four executive views — Overview, Demographics, Performance Tracker, and Attrition. Built on a star-schema data model with DAX measures for attrition rate, tenure cohorts, and rating time-intelligence. **Tech Stack:** Power BI, DAX, Power Query, Data Modeling --- ## 💼 Professional Experience ### 🔄 Digital Transformation Intern | Almansour Automotive *August 2025 · Giza, Egypt* — [Certificate ↗](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1r9tqi6OWlP2_XQrcC5Roxa3Fraq0A6BC/view?usp=sharing) - Rotated across IT, SAP, Digital Transformation, and Network Security departments - Built data pipeline in Microsoft Fabric with PySpark for ML-driven insights - Analyzed ERP workflows in SAP for digital process optimization - Collaborated on infrastructure management and data security initiatives ### 💻 Information Technology Intern | Arab Organization for Industrialization *July 2024 · Cairo, Egypt* - Completed [Internet of Things training (7 days)](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ZE03YaHaeHq7BTm4o4JEWdvxuFJys7rx/view?usp=sharing) — sensors, microcontrollers, practical applications - Completed [Cloud Computing training (5 days)](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aKzwmLgJAEgvrWwyC10t6Dd-IJuXMdJQ?usp=sharing) — infrastructure and deployment models --- ## 🎓 Certifications & Training **19 verified certificates** across DataCamp, WE Telecom, MCIT/DEPI, and Coursera. Full list with verification links is on the [portfolio site](https://my-portfolio-weld-eta-67.vercel.app/#certifications). ### DataCamp — 14 tracks **Career tracks:** - 📊 [Associate Data Scientist](https://www.datacamp.com/completed/statement-of-accomplishment/track/bd301feefedc66ab6876c15ab3f3d66ce79e117c) (90 hrs) - 🐍 [Associate Python Developer](https://www.datacamp.com/completed/statement-of-accomplishment/track/ab21d3bd8187ef037093d9b6a550481457bad13c) (32 hrs) - 🐍 [Python Developer](https://www.datacamp.com/completed/statement-of-accomplishment/track/5f6dcd54740393e9c14d92b9390380f797052853) (28 hrs) - 🔬 [Data Scientist](https://www.datacamp.com/completed/statement-of-accomplishment/track/367ca626806cd0f749cd1626e2f3c20157692d9b) (26 hrs) **Skill tracks:** - 🐍 [Python Data Fundamentals](https://www.datacamp.com/completed/statement-of-accomplishment/track/c60605375c21950ad26a0032f58df1c5146d8bc9) (28 hrs) - 🤖 [Applied Machine Learning in Python](https://coursera.org/verify/BUYFU9ADFMIQ) — *University of Michigan* - ☕ [Database Management with Java and SQL](https://coursera.org/verify/3P34H669GT2I) — *Amazon* --- ## 🌟 Achievements & Activities - 🏆 GPA: 3.8/4.0 in Computer Science (Software Engineering) - 🌍 COP27 Youth Volunteer (via KSIU) - 💻 Active participant in IEEE, GDSC, and ICPC student communities - 🎓 Huawei ICT Academy participant --- ## 📫 Let's Connect! I'm always open to collaborating on interesting data science and machine learning projects, or discussing opportunities in AI and analytics!

linkedin_signals.name: Zeyad Ahmed Elshenawy
linkedin_signals.about:
I’m passionate about leveraging Artificial Intelligence and Data Science to transform raw data into meaningful insights and real-world impact. My focus areas include machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, and data analytics, with growing interest in how these technologies can drive innovation across finance, education, and intelligent systems.

I enjoy the entire process of building intelligent solutions — from data collection and engineering to model design, training, and deployment. I approach problems like a scientist, exploring and experimenting, yet think like an engineer, ensuring every solution is scalable, efficient, and explainable.

Currently, I’m developing AI-driven prediction and sentiment analysis models that analyze market behavior and enhance decision-making. These experiences have strengthened my technical foundation in Python, SQL, and modern ML frameworks, as well as my ability to turn complex data into clear, actionable insights.

My goal is to keep building impactful, data-driven systems, contributing to teams that value innovation, and advancing the role of AI in shaping smarter, more adaptive technologies. … more

designations.skills: Machine Learning, Python (Programming Language) and +3 skills
designations.description:
Selected participant in the national Digital Egypt Pioneers Initiative (DEPI) — AI & Data Science track designed by IBM in partnership with Egypt’s Ministry of Communications & Information Technology (MCIT). Applied the full data-science lifecycle to real problems: data collection and cleaning, exploratory data analysis, feature engineering, model development and evaluation, and production-oriented deployment.

I developed practical skills in the Python data-science ecosystem and SQL, and used MLOps tools (MLflow, Hugging Face) to build reproducible pipelines. I practised prompt engineering and integrated AI tools for automation and model augmentation. Throughout the program I focused on translating data into actionable business insights to improve performance, increase efficiency, and enhance customer experience.

Core coursework and hands-on modules included:
• Prompt Engineering
• What is Data Science? / Data Science Methodology
• Tools for Data Science
• Python for Data Science, AI & Development + Python Project
• Databases & SQL for Data Science with Python
• Data Analysis, Visualization & Machine Learning with Python
• MLOps tools (MLflow, Hugging Face)
• Capstone project: end-to-end AI project demonstrating preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection, evaluation and deployment.

Forward-looking emphasis: reproducible, ethical, and scalable ML/AI solutions that bridge technical results and business impact.

designations.skills: Computer Information Systems, Python (Programming Language) and +5 skills
designations.description:
Rotational internship within the company’s Digital Transformation Division, gaining cross-functional exposure to IT, SAP ERP, Digital Transformation Strategy, and Network Security.

Contributed to modernization initiatives by designing a data pipeline using PySpark for large-scale data ingestion and preprocessing, and experimenting with machine learning models to support operational analytics and decision-making.

Analyzed ERP workflows to identify automation and efficiency opportunities, assisted in infrastructure monitoring and management, and collaborated with multidisciplinary teams driving the company’s digitalization roadmap.

Key Achievements:

Developed and optimized a PySpark-based data pipeline integrated with enterprise systems.

Participated in SAP process analysis and business workflow mapping for digital improvement.

Supported network infrastructure assessments and IT performance monitoring.

Applied ML techniques to extract insights from operational data.
